title: Tool Execution Pipeline
description: Batch tool invocation: fan-out execution, approval gating, result collection, error handling per tool
key_classes: _FunctionToolBatchExecutor: owns mutable state for a batch of function-tool executions; manages task lifecycle, failure arbitration, and result collection
FunctionTool: dataclass – name, description, params_json_schema, on_invoke_tool, needs_approval, failure_error_function, timeout, timeout_behavior
FunctionToolResult: dataclass – tool, output, run_item (ToolCallOutputItem | ToolApprovalItem | None), interruptions, agent_run_result
ToolApprovalItem: run item surfaced in RunResult.interruptions when a tool call is pending human review
RunContextWrapper: holds approval state; get_approval_status(), approve_tool(), reject_tool(), get_rejection_message()
ToolContext: per-call context derived from RunContextWrapper; carries tool_call, tool_namespace, agent, run_config
_FunctionToolTaskState: per-task mutable state; in_post_invoke_phase flag, invoke_task reference
activity_flow: START: execute_function_tool_calls() called with bindings, tool_runs[], hooks, context_wrapper, config
CREATE: _FunctionToolBatchExecutor instantiated; isolate_parallel_failures defaults to True when >1 tool
FAN-OUT: for each ToolRunFunction in tool_runs → asyncio.create_task(_run_single_tool())
DRAIN LOOP: asyncio.wait(pending_tasks, FIRST_COMPLETED) until all done
Partition siblings: cancellable_tasks (not in post_invoke) vs post_invoke_tasks
COLLECT: _build_function_tool_results() → assemble FunctionToolResult list with nested interruptions
IF needs approval=False → continue to tool body
IF approval_status is None (no stored decision) → return FunctionToolResult(run_item=ToolApprovalItem) [INTERRUPT]
IF approval_status is False (rejected) → resolve_approval_rejection_message() → return rejection FunctionToolResult
IF approval_status is True (approved) → continue to tool body
INPUT GUARDRAILS: _execute_tool_input_guardrails() → if rejected return rejection message
approval_gating_detail: needs_approval on FunctionTool: bool | async callable(context, parsed_args, call_id) → bool
Evaluation: function_needs_approval() parses JSON args, calls evaluate_needs_approval_setting()
Stored approval state lives in RunContextWrapper; keyed by (tool_name, call_id, tool_namespace, tool_lookup_key)
States: None=pending (interrupt), True=approved (proceed), False=rejected (return rejection message)
ToolApprovalItem carries: agent, raw_item, tool_name, tool_namespace, tool_origin, tool_lookup_key
After interruption: caller calls state.approve(item) or state.reject(item, rejection_message=...)
always_approve=True / always_reject=True sticky decisions persist across resumed run turns
Rejection message resolution order: per-call rejection_message > RunConfig.tool_error_formatter > REJECTION_MESSAGE constant
parallel_failure_isolation: isolate_parallel_failures=True (default for batch >1): sibling tasks are cancelled on first failure
Partition tasks: cancellable (not in post_invoke phase) vs post_invoke (already past tool invocation)
Cancellable tasks get asyncio.cancel(); waited via _drain_cancelled_tasks()
Post-invoke tasks get a fixed timeout (_FUNCTION_TOOL_POST_INVOKE_WAIT_SECONDS)
Late failures from teardown are merged via _merge_late_function_tool_failure() using priority ordering
_get_function_tool_failure_priority: ranks ToolInputGuardrailTripwireTriggered > ToolOutputGuardrailTripwireTriggered > other AgentsExceptions > generic
guardrails_detail: ToolInputGuardrailTripwireTriggered / ToolOutputGuardrailTripwireTriggered exceptions propagate as high-priority failures
nested_agent_tool_results: Agent.as_tool() results stored as nested run state keyed by tool_call in RunContextWrapper scope
_resolve_nested_tool_run_result(): peek nested run result; consume if no unresolved interruptions
_consume_nested_tool_run_result(): consume agent tool state via consume_agent_tool_run_result()
Nested interruptions (ToolApprovalItem list) bubble up to FunctionToolResult.interruptions
If nested interruptions unresolved: run_item=None (suppress tool output until resolved)
key_relationships: FunctionToolResult contains run_item: ToolCallOutputItem | ToolApprovalItem | None
ToolApprovalItem surfaces in RunResult.interruptions for human-in-the-loop
error_handling_paths: Tool invocation exception → UserError wrapping unless already AgentsException
Timeout message returned to model if timeout_behavior='error_as_result' (default)
failure_error_function on FunctionTool handles invocation crashes; None=re-raise; default=default_tool_error_function
Parent cancellation: all pending tasks cancelled, done callbacks log remaining exceptions
important_notes: Fan-out is true asyncio parallelism via create_task per tool. Approval gating is per-call, stored in RunContextWrapper keyed by call_id. Nested Agent.as_tool() interruptions surface on outer run. Post-invoke phase (after tool returns but before hooks/guardrails finish) is protected from cancellation during sibling failure teardown.
